title: aerides cootesii
intro: Aerides cootesii, also known as the Cootes' orchid, is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to relieve coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems, and its leaves and flowers are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Aerides cootesii include infusions and decoctions, which are made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea that can be used to treat various health issues.
